topic_groups.topic: Health Care Access, Medication Management and Safety

articles.title: Enlisting pharmacists and nurse practitioners in medication management can fill critical gaps
articles.summary_3_sentences: An economic model projected that adding pharmacist- and nurse practitioner-led medication management to heart failure care could help patients live longer and spend less time in the hospital. The study, reported in the Canadian Journal of Cardiology, also found the approach could be cost-effective for the health care system.

articles.title: Survey finds 3 in 10 young adults don't have a doctor; if they do, most don't see them
articles.summary_3_sentences: A survey commissioned by The Ohio State University Wexner Medical Center found that three in 10 young adults do not have a primary care doctor. The report highlights concerns that many young adults may be missing preventive care and other benefits of an ongoing primary care relationship.

articles.title: Hospital discharge sedatives linked to more falls, readmissions and deaths in older adults
articles.summary_3_sentences: Research published in the Canadian Medical Association Journal linked new sedative prescriptions at hospital discharge with higher risks of falls and other adverse outcomes in older adults. The reported concerns were especially notable for benzodiazepines and antipsychotics.

articles.title: Melatonin can be a safe and effective sleep aid for all ages but improper dosing leads to real harms
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article discusses melatonin use as a sleep aid for both children and adults amid conflicting public messages about safety. It emphasizes that dosing and appropriate use matter, because improper dosing can cause real harms.

articles.title: New button battery technology shows promise, but swallowing remains an emergency
articles.summary_3_sentences: A peer-reviewed assessment of a child-safety lithium coin-cell battery found that the titanium-based design may reduce the severity of tissue injury after ingestion. Researchers cautioned that swallowing a button battery remains a medical emergency and management protocols should not change yet.

articles.title: Lecanemab for Alzheimer's disease: Discussing risks and benefits is key
articles.summary_3_sentences: Following Canada's approval of lecanemab for early Alzheimer's disease, an article in the Canadian Medical Association Journal reviewed its modest clinical benefits, risks of serious adverse effects, and treatment complexity. The article recommends shared decision-making with patients and caregivers about potential benefits and harms.

articles.title: Researchers discover a cause of neuron excitability in ALS, suggesting a new potential treatment
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers at Northwestern Medicine's Les Turner ALS Center reported molecular findings that may explain why nerve cells overfire in ALS. They also designed a drug intended to stop this overfiring, but the report describes a potential treatment direction rather than established clinical benefit.

articles.title: Common pesticide linked to more than double the risk of Parkinson's disease
articles.summary_3_sentences: UCLA scientists linked long-term residential exposure to chlorpyrifos with more than double the risk of Parkinson's disease. Laboratory studies described in the report found that chlorpyrifos can damage dopamine neurons and disrupt clearance of toxic protein buildup.

articles.title: VR study with zebrafish shows surroundings influence developing biology of the eye
articles.summary_3_sentences: A zebrafish study using virtual-reality-like surroundings found that the environment experienced during development can affect eye neurons. The changes involved neuron shape, electrical activity, and later behavior, making this a preclinical developmental biology finding.

articles.title: Neural pathways reveal a push-pull system for coordinating goal-directed behavior in mice
articles.summary_3_sentences: A mouse study examined neural pathways involved in coordinating movement and progress toward goals. The work focused on basal ganglia circuits and described a push-pull system for goal-directed behavior in mice.

articles.title: Fatal rabies case with no visible wound shows why bat contact alone should trigger immediate vaccination, experts say
articles.summary_3_sentences: A Canadian Medical Association Journal article reviewed Ontario's first fatal rabies case since 1967. Experts said the case underscores that bat contact alone, even without a visible wound, should prompt immediate rabies vaccination evaluation.

articles.title: Proteins Help Deadly Pathogen Evade Immunity
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article describes research on louse-borne relapsing fever, an acute bacterial infection that causes recurring high fevers. It focuses on proteins that may help the pathogen evade immune defenses.

articles.title: A COVID-19 Preventive Strategy is Now FDA Approved
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article reports that a COVID-19 preventive strategy has received FDA approval. It frames the approval in the context of continued efforts to prevent and treat COVID-19 after the pandemic's global impact.

articles.title: A Natural Antifungal Mechanism is Revealed
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article discusses a newly described natural mechanism affecting Aspergillus fumigatus, a common environmental fungus that people frequently inhale. The finding is presented as basic or mechanistic research relevant to antifungal defense.

articles.title: Outbreak Updates: Measles in the US, Ebola in the DRC
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article provides outbreak updates on measles in the United States and Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo. It reports continued rapid spread of measles in the United States, including a cited total of 2,288 confirmed cases.

articles.title: Gut Fungi are Linked to Asthma Development
articles.summary_3_sentences: Research reported in Nature Communications highlighted the possible importance of fungi in the gut microbiome for asthma development. The article presents the finding as a link involving the microbiome rather than proof that gut fungi cause asthma in humans.

articles.title: Genetic Variants that Cause IBD Point to Potential Treatment
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article discusses genetic variants implicated in inflammatory bowel disease, including ulcerative colitis and Crohn's disease. It reports that these variants may point researchers toward potential treatment strategies.

articles.title: Researchers Find a Cause of Inflammatory Bowel Disease
articles.summary_3_sentences: Scientists reported new insights into inflammatory bowel disease, a group of gastrointestinal disorders that includes Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis. The article describes the work as identifying a cause or contributor to disease biology, with details limited in the provided record.

articles.title: New Insights Into the Link Between Colon Cancer and a Bacterial Toxin
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article examines links between colon cancer and a toxin associated with Bacteroides fragilis, a common gut bacterium. It presents the work as research into how gut bacteria and bacterial toxins may relate to human disease.

articles.title: Eight Common Food Additives Linked to Heart Disease
articles.summary_3_sentences: Researchers analyzed dietary intake data and cardiovascular outcomes and identified eight common food additives associated with increased heart disease risk. The reported findings are correlational and support further evaluation of additive exposure and cardiovascular health.

articles.title: Scientists uncover how ovarian cancer resists chemotherapy--and how to reverse it
articles.summary_3_sentences: Michigan State University researchers identified a mechanism by which ovarian cancer cells become resistant to chemotherapy. They also reported that blocking a specific protein could restore drug effectiveness, based on the provided research summary.

articles.title: Combination Therapy Shows Promise for Hormone-Receptor Positive Breast Cancer
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article reports on a combination therapy approach for hormone-receptor-positive breast cancer. The provided summary notes the high burden of breast cancer but gives limited details on the treatment regimen or study stage.

articles.title: GLP-1 Drugs Linked to 35% Lower Risk of Breast Cancer
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article reports that women taking GLP-1 drugs such as Ozempic, Wegovy, and Mounjaro were less likely to develop breast cancer in the cited analysis. The finding is described as an association and should not be interpreted as proving the drugs prevent breast cancer.

articles.title: Combination Chemotherapy and Immunotherapy Treatment for Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article discusses treatment involving chemotherapy and immunotherapy for non-small cell lung cancer. It notes that risk of NSCLC increases with age and presents the combination approach in the context of lung cancer therapy.

articles.title: National Cancer Survivors Day Celebrates Survivorship
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article marks National Cancer Survivors Day, an observance honoring people with a history of cancer. It focuses on cancer survivorship rather than reporting a specific clinical trial or laboratory finding.

articles.title: Calcium and Vitamin D Don't Prevent Bone Fractures
articles.summary_3_sentences: A recent analysis examined whether calcium and vitamin D supplementation reduces bone fracture risk. The study found no significant reduction in fracture incidence among participants, challenging broad supplementation assumptions for fracture prevention.

articles.title: Higher Tyrosine in Men Linked to Shorter Lifespan
articles.summary_3_sentences: An epidemiological study found that higher blood tyrosine levels in men were associated with shorter lifespan and increased mortality. The analysis reportedly controlled for factors such as age and lifestyle, but the finding remains associative.

articles.title: Bones communicate with the rest of the body to support overall health: Here's the science behind your skeleton
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article explains how bones are continuously maintained and repaired, noting that millions of fractures are treated each year in the United States. It also describes the skeleton as biologically active and connected to broader aspects of health.

articles.title: Only one workout helped older adults lose fat without losing muscle
articles.summary_3_sentences: A six-month study of more than 120 adults in their 70s compared exercise approaches for changes in fat and lean mass. High-intensity interval training helped reduce body fat while preserving muscle mass, whereas moderate and low-intensity workouts also led to some fat loss but did not show the same lean-mass preservation.

articles.title: Breastfeeding may help babies sleep longer by age one, challenging formula feeding claims
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article addresses the common perception that formula-fed infants sleep longer than breastfed infants. It reports findings suggesting breastfeeding may help babies sleep longer by age one, while also noting established WHO recommendations for exclusive breastfeeding during the first six months.

articles.title: Reproduction affects health--and so does biological sex
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article discusses research into how reproductive history, pregnancy, biological sex, and partner genetics may relate to later health. It emphasizes that scientists are still beginning to understand how pregnancy affects health over the life course.

topic_groups.topic: Genomics, Drug Discovery and Translational Biomanufacturing

articles.title: Genome Mapping Nearly Complete for the Pangenome
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article reports progress toward completing pangenome mapping, which aims to capture genetic variation across populations rather than relying on a single reference genome. It frames the pangenome as a broad catalog of human genetic information.

articles.title: Traditional Chinese Herb Shows Promise for Hair Loss
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article reports that Polygonum multiflorum, a root used in traditional Chinese medicine, may have potential for male and female pattern hair loss. The provided record does not specify whether the evidence is preclinical or clinical, so the finding should be treated as preliminary.

articles.title: Space Plants Could Be Future Pharmacies for Astronauts
articles.summary_3_sentences: The article discusses research on whether plants could be used to produce pharmaceuticals for future astronauts. The study is framed as space-focused translational biomanufacturing rather than an immediately available medical application.

articles.title: Sun-like star 'eats' planet in rare cosmic event
articles.summary_3_sentences: Astronomers observed a rare event in which a sun-like star consumed a planet. Changes in brightness and spectroscopic data suggested material from the planet was accreted onto the star, offering insight into planetary system evolution.
